benchmark_name: MILU: Multi-task Indic Language Understanding Benchmark
humanities_role_explanation: Uses humanities content as both evaluation material (regional examinations covering local history, arts, festivals, laws) and is designed specifically for culturally relevant humanities understanding in Indian languages
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Text-only multiple choice questions in 11 Indic languages covering culturally specific topics from regional examinations
analytical_tasks_explanation: Cultural knowledge evaluation requiring understanding of region-specific history, arts, festivals, laws, and traditions across Indian states; tests both linguistic competence and cultural understanding beyond simple factual recall

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Comprehensive benchmark spanning 8 domains and 41 subjects across multiple languages for evaluating LLM capabilities

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates 42+ LLMs including GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, Gemini-1.5-Pro, Gemini-1.5-Flash, Llama-3.1 series, Gemma-2 series, and various Indic language-specific models

llm_is_primary_system.note: LLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked for their cultural and linguistic understanding capabilities

arts_humanities_data.note: Language and Linguistics (1206), History, Literature and Literary Theory, Visual Arts and Performing Arts, Philosophy, Religious Studies - covers local history, arts, festivals, literature, music, performing arts

quantitative_metrics.note: Accuracy scores across languages, domains, and shot settings (0-shot, 1-shot, 5-shot)

english_language.note: Paper written in English, benchmark includes English alongside 10 Indic languages

summary: MILU introduces the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LLMs on culturally relevant knowledge in 11 Indic languages, covering 8 domains and 41 subjects with questions sourced from regional Indian examinations. The benchmark specifically tests cultural understanding of local history, arts, festivals, and laws rather than just linguistic capability.
key_findings: GPT-4o achieved highest accuracy at 74%. Open multilingual models outperformed language-specific fine-tuned models. Models performed significantly worse in culturally relevant domains (Arts & Humanities, Social Sciences) compared to STEM fields, highlighting lack of cultural knowledge in current models.
paper_name: Fine-tuning Large Language Models for Automated Pun Translation
